verses.text: In the last days the mountain of the LORD's house shall be exalted, and nations shall stream to it to learn His ways, beating swords into plowshares. The gospel age is foreseen. Through Christ the nations come to God and learn the way of peace.

In the last days the mountain of the LORD's house shall be exalted, and nations shall stream to it to learn His ways, beating swords into plowshares. The gospel age is foreseen. Through Christ the nations come to God and learn the way of peace.

Isaiah urges Jacob to walk in the LORD's light, for the land is full of idols, silver, and the worship of men's own handiwork. They have forsaken God for things they made. Idolatry, ancient or modern, bows to what cannot save.

Men are bidden to hide in the rock from the dread of the LORD, for 'the lofty looks of man shall be humbled.' Pride cannot stand before God. The day comes when human haughtiness is brought low and He alone is exalted.

Men are bidden to hide in the rock from the dread of the LORD, for 'the lofty looks of man shall be humbled.' Pride cannot stand before God. The day comes when human haughtiness is brought low and He alone is exalted.

The day of the LORD is against all that is proud and lifted up — cedars, mountains, towers, and ships of Tarshish. Everything men trust in will be shaken. No earthly greatness can withstand the judgment of God.

Man's loftiness shall be bowed, idols abolished, and men shall flee to caves 'for fear of the LORD.' The LORD alone shall be exalted that day. When He arises to judge, every false confidence collapses.

Men will fling their idols to the moles and bats; 'cease ye from man, whose breath is in his nostrils.' Trust in man and idols alike is folly. The frail creature is no one to lean on; only God is.
